commit.message:
Add timezone-aware email delivery for all cron jobs

- Migration 040 adds timezone column to customers and vendors tables
- Browser timezone captured on signup (user metadata) and login (API body)
- Timezone updated on every login to handle moves/travel
- Crons run hourly; each checks customer's local time before sending:
  - Expiration reminders: send at 9 AM or 7 PM local
  - Review requests: send at 9 AM or 10 AM local
- Default timezone: America/New_York for existing users

commit.message:
Redesign signal click: three-column intelligence workspace replaces drawer

Replace the cramped 480px slide-out drawer with a full-width three-column
workspace layout (25/50/25) that activates when clicking any signal:

- LEFT: Signal navigation panel with same-category, cross-category, and
  developing signals for quick switching without returning to feed
- CENTER: Expanded intelligence brief with headline, lede, intel note,
  signals, markets, probability, score, and decision workflow
- RIGHT: Hero probability display, sparkline, related developing markets,
  cross-sector signals, and platform source links

Includes responsive breakpoints: 2-column at ≤1080px, single-column at
≤640px. Smooth fade-in animation, Escape key to close, back button nav.

docs: add Palantir-class readiness assessment, roadmap, and Canonical Graph Contract v1 (#19090)

### Motivation
- Lock a graph-first, evidence-first execution baseline to prevent
architectural drift and compress feedback loops.
- Provide a prioritized, phase-based implementation roadmap and a
12-component capability assessment to guide work and acceptance
criteria.

### Description
- Add `docs/architecture/canonical-graph-contract-v1.md` which defines
primitives (`Entity`, `Event`, `Relationship`, `Evidence`, `Claim`,
`Narrative`), required fields, policy invariants, versioning, and
migration discipline.
- Add `docs/roadmap/palantir-class-assessment.md` with the 12-component
readiness assessment, status, confidence, and immediate mandates.
- Add `docs/roadmap/palantir-class-roadmap.md` with the phased (Phase
0–10) execution plan, deliverables, exit criteria, a 7-day
risk-reduction plan, and MAESTRO alignment.
- Update `docs/roadmap/STATUS.json` to register the
`palantir-class-readiness-program` initiative and update the revision
note/timestamp.

### Testing
- Validated `docs/roadmap/STATUS.json` is well-formed JSON using `python
-m json.tool docs/roadmap/STATUS.json`, which succeeded.
- Ran `git diff --check` to surface whitespace/merge problems, which
returned no issues.

* Add flavor guidance, cooking skill adaptation, and dietary prefs to AI recipe prompts

Recipes generated by the AI lacked flavor depth because prompts contained
zero taste guidance and ignored the user's cooking skill level. This adds
a FLAVOR_GUIDANCE block (salt/acid/fat/heat/umami balance, layered seasoning,
finishing elements, proper cooking techniques) to all recipe generation,
meal plan, and meal swap prompts. Also wires the user's cookingSkill,
dietary preferences, and excluded ingredients into recipe generation and
meal swap constraint blocks where they were previously missing.

commit.message:
fix(kube): extend demo_verifier login retry budget and reduce connect timeout (#475)

Each login attempt was blocked by the default 30s TCP connect timeout,
exhausting the 5-attempt retry budget before the API pod was ready.

Reduce connect timeout to 5s and retry every 5s for up to 60 attempts
(~5 min total). This gives ample headroom for the API pod to start
during Helm upgrades.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(structcorr): add deterministic structural correctness framework and CI gate (#19089)

### Motivation

- Reduce structural hallucinations by making structural correctness a
deterministic, machine-checkable gate for model outputs
(JSON/SQL/Markdown tables/LaTeX).
- Capture FireRed-OCR lessons (format-constrained validation /
reward-hook surface) as a non-training evaluation and evidence
framework.
- Provide an operational, auditable path (artifacts, drift tracking, CI
gate) so structural contract violations can fail CI and be monitored
over time.

### Description

- Add a new Python package `src/summit/structcorr` with a `runner` and
`evidence` writer that emits deterministic artifacts
(`artifacts/structcorr/report.json`, `metrics.json`, `stamp.json`) and
stable evidence IDs.
- Implement per-format validators under
`src/summit/structcorr/validators/` for JSON (`json_contract.py`), SQL
(`sql_parse.py`), Markdown table integrity (`md_table.py`), and LaTeX
safe-mode checks (`latex_compile.py`), and wire them into
`run_structcorr`; validators run deny-by-default and LaTeX blocks
dangerous primitives.
- Add a feature flag `SUMMIT_STRUCTCORR` (default `0`) to keep the
framework off by default, plus helper scripts and automation:
`scripts/dev/repo_reality_check.py`,
`scripts/monitoring/structcorr-drift.py`,
`scripts/profiling/structcorr_profile.py`, `project_wbs/structcorr.md`,
and docs under `docs/standards/` and `docs/security/data-handling/` and
`docs/ops/runbooks/structcorr.md`.
- Add targeted pytest coverage under `tests/structcorr/` and a scoped CI
workflow `.github/workflows/structcorr.yml` that runs the validators and
generates smoke artifacts for drift/profile monitoring.

### Testing

- Ran unit tests: `pytest -q tests/structcorr/test_json_contracts.py`,
`pytest -q tests/structcorr/test_sql_parse.py`, `pytest -q
tests/structcorr/test_md_table_integrity.py`, and `pytest -q
tests/structcorr/test_latex_sandbox_compile.py`, and all tests passed.
- Exercised the end-to-end smoke/profile path with `SUMMIT_STRUCTCORR=1`
and `python scripts/profiling/structcorr_profile.py` plus `python
scripts/monitoring/structcorr-drift.py`, which produced deterministic
artifacts under `artifacts/structcorr/`.
- Ran the repo reality check with `python
scripts/dev/repo_reality_check.py` to generate
`docs/repo_assumptions.md` and validated artifact generation and metric
aggregation succeeded.

------
[Codex
Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_e_69a7e1883cf48333a17f958e17b720c0)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Introduced structural correctness validation framework supporting JSON
schema, SQL structure, Markdown table integrity, and LaTeX safe-mode
checks.
* Added monitoring and profiling capabilities for validation metrics and
performance tracking.

* **Documentation**
* Added operational runbooks and data-handling guidelines for structural
correctness framework.
* Documented validation standards, security constraints, and work
breakdown structure.

* **Tests**
  * Added test coverage for all validation validators.

commit.message:
perf: increase exploration timeouts and output budget for reliability

Addresses Gap 5 (32% exploration failure rate) from alignment roadmap:
- Raise exploration timeout from 120s → 180s (timeouts.ts)
- Raise per-CLI timeout from 60s → 90s (parallel-exploration-types.ts)
- Raise output chars per CLI from 4k → 8k (parallel-exploration-types.ts)

Exploration tasks involve recursive codebase traversal that often exceeded
the previous timeout limits. Larger output budget prevents truncation of
findings. Updated 3 test assertions for new defaults.

commit.message:
Architecture overhaul: Skills, Lobster workflows, channel separation, anti-self-approval

Major changes:
- Add 5 OpenClaw Skills (email-read, email-send, calendar-read, calendar-create, startup)
  replacing ad-hoc exec patterns with documented, repeatable workflows
- Add email-triage Lobster workflow for batched inbox processing with single approval gate
- Configure approval channel separation (prompts go to Dave's private Telegram, not agent channel)
- Expand allowlist with glob patterns covering both script locations + basic shell tools
- Add safeBins config for grep/cat/ls (stops them triggering approval)
- Add Gmail PubSub integration docs (push-based email, eliminates polling)
- Add self-approval prevention rules throughout AGENTS.md, TOOLS.md, telegram.md
- Add AMBER-SETUP-INSTRUCTIONS.md with step-by-step machine-specific setup
- Enforce sequential command execution (no parallel reads/approval spam)

commit.message:
fix: switch DB driver from @vercel/postgres to pg for Prisma Postgres compatibility

@vercel/postgres uses Neon's WebSocket protocol which is incompatible with
Prisma Postgres (db.prisma.io). Switch to node-postgres (pg) with a tagged
template helper — same interface, works with any standard Postgres connection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(agents): initial summit multi-agent orchestrator (#19088)

### Motivation
- Provide a deterministic, test-backed orchestration runtime for Summit
agents to avoid CI gridlock and enforce predictable
planner→builder→reviewer→governance flows.
- Introduce a minimal production-ready execution layer so specialized
agents (Jules, Codex, Observer, Antigravity) can be registered, routed,
and observed under a single deterministic flow.
- Deliver queue-level guardrails (priority, bounded retries,
concurrency) to isolate failures and ensure predictable task scheduling.

### Description
- Added a role-driven orchestrator
`agents/orchestrator/agent-orchestrator.ts` that registers agents and
runs the deterministic flow `planner -> builder -> reviewer ->
governance` and exposes queue snapshotting and process methods.
- Implemented a reusable `TaskQueue` in `agents/queue/task-queue.ts`
with priority scheduling, FIFO tie-break, configurable concurrency, and
bounded retry/retry-exhaustion semantics.
- Added baseline agent implementations: `agents/jules-planner.ts`,
`agents/codex-builder.ts`, `agents/observer-reviewer.ts`, and
`agents/antigravity-governance.ts` that illustrate expected role
contracts and wiring.
- Added orchestration tests at `tests/agents/orchestrator.test.ts`
covering agent registration, end-to-end routing, priority scheduling,
and retry recovery, and wired test execution into the PR gating workflow
(`.github/workflows/pr-gate.yml`); also updated
`docs/roadmap/STATUS.json` to track the initiative.

### Testing
- Ran the orchestration test suite with `pnpm exec tsx --test
tests/agents/orchestrator.test.ts` which passed (4 tests, all passed).
- Ran automated linting with `pnpm exec eslint --no-ignore ...` and
`node scripts/check-boundaries.cjs`; both completed (ESLint produced
warnings only, boundary check passed).
- Attempted `pnpm exec jest --runInBand
tests/agents/orchestrator.test.ts` earlier but it failed in this
environment due to the repository Jest setup referencing a missing local
setup file; the CI step was adjusted to run the `tsx`-based tests which
are green.

------
[Codex
Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_e_69a89c0b1604832e94f2602f1c489a75)

commit.message:
feat(agents): skill registry + policy enforcement (default-deny) (#19087)

### Motivation

- Provide a deterministic, unit-testable default-deny Skill Registry +
ABAC-lite policy layer so Summit agents can only invoke explicitly
permitted skills.
- Centralize enforcement at the orchestrator edge and emit
governance-grade allow/deny/provenance events for every invocation to
support auditing and rollback decisions.

### Description

- Added core types in `summit/agents/skills/types.ts`: `SkillSpec`,
`SkillInvocation`, `PolicyDecision`, and `SkillName`.
- Implemented a single-source `SkillRegistry` with `register`, `get`,
and `list` plus starter skills (`plan.generate`, `repo.read`,
`repo.write`, `tests.run`, `ci.inspect`, `release.approve`) in
`summit/agents/skills/registry.ts`.
- Introduced policy format, JSON schema, example YAML and
loader/normalizer in `summit/agents/policy/` (`policy.schema.json`,
`policy.example.yml`, `load-policy.ts`) with schema validation via
`ajv`.
- Implemented deterministic ABAC-lite evaluator in
`summit/agents/policy/evaluate.ts` that matches `agent_names` / `roles`
/ `skills` / `envs` and optional `repo_paths_glob` / `dataset_ids` /
`connector_ids`, with precedence: explicit deny > allow > default deny.
- Enforced policy in a new orchestrator API
`AgentOrchestrator.invokeSkill(...)` in
`summit/agents/orchestrator/agent-orchestrator.ts`, which evaluates
policy before execution, short-circuits denied calls, and emits events
`SKILL_DENIED`, `SKILL_ALLOWED`, `SKILL_EXEC_STARTED`,
`SKILL_EXEC_FINISHED` including `run_id`, `task_id`, `agent_name`,
`skill`, `decision`, `reason`, `inputs_hash`, `outputs_hash`, and
`matched_rules`.
- Added deterministic helper hashing (`stableHash`/`stableStringify`)
for inputs/outputs and a dev demo script
`summit/agents/demo/run-skill-demo.ts` plus npm script
`orchestrate:skills-demo` and README snippet `summit/agents/README.md`.

### Testing

- Unit tests: ran `pnpm tsx --test tests/agents/policy-evaluate.test.ts
tests/agents/skill-registry.test.ts
tests/agents/orchestrator-skill-enforcement.test.ts` and all tests
passed (policy evaluation, registry behavior, deny-overrides-allow, repo
glob constraints, schema validation, and orchestrator event sequence).
- Integration/demo: executed `pnpm orchestrate:skills-demo` which showed
an allowed invocation and a denied invocation and printed the event +
provenance tails successfully.
- Safety checks: ran `node scripts/check-boundaries.cjs` to verify
module boundary constraints and it passed (no violations).

commit.message:
feat(indexer): scaffold event indexer with REST API

Add packages/indexer — a lightweight service that syncs all
AegisRegistry contract events into SQLite (via sql.js WASM)
and exposes them through a Hono REST API on port 4200.

Endpoints: /skills, /skills/unaudited, /skills/:hash,
/auditors/leaderboard, /auditors/:commitment, /disputes/open,
/disputes/:id, /bounties/open, /stats, /stats/events.

Sync engine: chunked historical backfill from deployment block
+ live polling every 5s. Handles all 10 contract events.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: schooladmin report-table show endpointinə giriş icazəsi

GET /api/report-tables/{table} yalnız report_tables.read tələb edirdi.
schooladmin-in isə bu permissonu yoxdur, yalnız report_table_responses.write var.
show endpoint-ini qrupdan çıxarıb OR middleware tətbiq edildi:
report_tables.read|report_table_responses.write

analytics endpoint-i ilə eyni pattern — cədvələ cavab yaza bilirsənsə,
həmin cədvəlin detallarını (notes, columns, fixed_rows) da oxuya bilirsən.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: TIS
repository.description: Təhsil idarəetmə sistemi

commit.message:
Add polis shared infrastructure module (CDK)

Redesigned polis as a CDK-managed shared infrastructure hub replacing
the old CloudFormation-generated stacks. Agents access secrets directly
via IAM roles instead of going through proxy services.

- CDK stacks: cogent-polis (ECS, ECR, DynamoDB, watcher Lambda) and
  cogent-secrets (rotation Lambda, cross-account reader role)
- SecretStore client with TTL cache for direct Secrets Manager access
- Agent watcher Lambda polls CF/ECS/CloudWatch every 60s into DynamoDB
- Click CLI: polis create/update/destroy/status, secrets, cogents list
- Migrated secrets to cogent/{name}/{channel} and polis/shared/{key}
- Removed boundary/proxy layer (Envoy, auth injector, WS proxy)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Switch to live API-based usage tracking via Anthropic rate limit headers

Replace local JSONL file parsing with real-time API calls that read
Anthropic's rate limit headers, matching exactly what claude.ai/settings/usage
shows. Now tracks ALL Claude usage (Chat, Cowork, Code) not just Code.

- Read OAuth token from macOS Keychain (no hardcoded keys)
- Minimal API call (~9 tokens/min via Haiku) to get utilization %
- Show reset countdowns and status indicators (allowed/warning/limited)
- Remove token limit config (API provides real percentages)
- Add Security.framework for Keychain access

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
